title: Automation Systems

content.question: What repetitive task are you doing manually that a tool could handle?
sop: List every task you repeat weekly with no real decision-making involved.
Count how many of those are already Automated versus your Total Tasks (see calculation, page 4)
Pick the single highest-frequency manual task and find or build a tool to automate it.
Set it up, test it for a week, and fix any errors it introduces.
Repeat monthly, as one task gets automated, promote the next-most-repetitive one.
questions: What repetitive task are you doing manually that a tool could handle?
If you got sick for a week, which repeated task would simply stop happening?
What's the last new tool you evaluated for something you still do by hand every day?
content.equation_example: Automated equals 6 tasks, Total Tasks equals 10. Freedom equals 60% out of 100%. Higher means more of your operation runs without you touching it. Lower means you're still the bottleneck in most of your own repetitive work.
content.closing_statement: Every task still done by hand is still a leash.

caption:
Principle Nº 144: Automation Systems

Remove yourself from repetitive tasks.

Ask yourself: What repetitive task are you doing manually that a tool could handle?
